news.headline: Money-market fund assets rise to $6.95t in the period to May 7th - ICI.

news.summary: US Foods Holding Corp. (NYSE: USFD) saw an increase in shares after releasing their first-quarter results. The company reported adjusted earnings per share of 68 cents, slightly below the analyst consensus of 69 cents. Their quarterly sales reached $9.35 billion, marking a 4.5% increase from the previous year, though falling short of the Street's expectation of $9.38 billion. Sysco experienced a 1.1% growth in total case volume, with a notable 2.5% boost from independent restaurants. Gross profit also saw a healthy rise, reaching $1.6 billion, up by 8%. Net income showed a substantial surge of 40.2%, hitting $115 million. For more details, the full article can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: US Foods' net income increased by 40.2%, reaching $115 million. The company plans a $1 billion buyback as shares rise.

news.summary: In the bustling city of Dallas, the prestigious Dallas Business Journal has recently recognized Suchi Reddy, the Chief Information Officer at Visual Matrix, a renowned company offering cutting-edge technology solutions for the hospitality sector, as an honoree for the esteemed 12th Annual Women in Technology Awards. This annual event acknowledges the accomplishments of 20 exceptional women in the Dallas business realm for their dedication to driving technological advancements in the area. With an impressive 25 years of experience in both the technology and hospitality fields, Reddy stands out as a seasoned professional making significant contributions to her industry.
news.headline: Suchi Reddy, Chief Information Officer at Visual Matrix, honored by Dallas Business Journal's Women in Technology Awards.

news.summary: XRP experienced a surge on May 8, driven by positive sentiment following Donald Trump's teased trade deal with the UK. The number of addresses holding 10,000+ XRP has been steadily increasing, indicating whale accumulation during price dips. XRP broke above a key resistance level, with a predicted rally towards $2.80-$3.66. XRP's price gained 7.50% to reach an intraday high of $2.27. Trump's trade deal announcement with the UK boosted risk appetite, reflected in a rise in S&P 500 futures and the broader crypto market. The 52-week correlation coefficient between XRP and the S&P 500 index has grown. Large holders accumulating XRP signals confidence for future gains. XRP's ongoing rally, breaking key resistance, suggests a potential 30% surge towards $2.80. A breakout above the upper trendline might push XRP towards $3.66. However, falling below $2.20 could lead to declines towards the 200-day EMA at $2.00. Adherence to technical analysis and risk management is advised in investment decisions.
news.headline: XRP surged 7.50% on May 8 to $2.27 amid trade deal buzz with the UK. Whale accumulation seen with rise in 10K+ XRP addresses. Bullish rally towards $2.80-$3.66 anticipated as XRP breaks key resistance. TradingView info reveals growing S&P correlation for XRP. Large holders confident for further gains. Whales continue to stack XRP despite pullbacks. Analysts predict an upside for XRP if it holds above $2.20 support. Editorial does not offer investment advice.

news.summary: Bitcoin price surged above $100,000, hitting $101,707, supported by positive regulatory and financial developments. US President Trump's trade deal announcement with the UK hinted at tariff reductions, leading to market optimism. The Dow rose 500 points, S&P 500 up 1.47%. Bitcoin traded around $101,600. Notably, $241 million in futures market liquidations initially contributed to the rally. Recent state bills in the US making Bitcoin reserves legal, and OCC confirming banks can trade crypto, indicate growing support. FDIC guideline allows banks to hold crypto assets. Bitcoin treasury and ETF inflows surged. Investors keen on BTC holding $100,000 support level for sustained growth. Market analysts emphasize monitoring BTC movements closely. Trump hinted at more trade deals, boosting market confidence. Bitcoin's return to six-figure territory signals improved investment and political environment since February. This reaffirms positive growth prospects for cryptocurrencies amidst evolving regulatory and market landscapes. Note: This summary does not offer investment advice. Investors should conduct individual research before making decisions.
news.headline: Bitcoin surpasses $101K amid US strategic reserve bill passing, BTC adoption surges. Market confident $100K will serve as strong support.

The situation reflects the complexity and potential consequences of governmental decisions in the tech industry.

news.summary: Connecting Patients with Specialized Care ANN ARBOR, Mich., May 8, 2025 - The Sarcoma Alliance for Research through Collaboration (SARC) has announced the release of an updated SARC Sarcoma Centers Directory (SCD). This directory offers extensive information on specialized care centers for sarcoma patients, providing access to key resources. The revitalized SCD now includes a wider range of facilities, with a total of 150 sarcoma centers across the country, up from the previous 100. This expansion aims to bridge gaps in patient care and improve accessibility to specialized treatments.
news.headline: SARC introduces enhanced Sarcoma Centers Directory at upcoming meeting, bridging gaps between patients and specialized care.

news.summary: Toronto Hydro Corporation reported its financial and operating results for the first quarter ending March 31, 2025. The distribution revenue stood at $240.2 million, up $15.0 million from the same period in 2024 due to higher distribution rates and electricity consumption. Net income after regulatory balances saw a significant increase to $53.2 million, a $19.3 million rise from the prior year, driven by higher revenue and lower net credit amounts, despite increased expenses. Capital expenditures also surged to $244.7 million, up $45.2 million, attributed to customer connections and infrastructure replacements. James Hinds resigned from the Board of Directors on March 19 for personal reasons. Full story on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Toronto Hydro Corp. reported Q1 2025 financial results: $240.2M distribution revenue, $53.2M net income, $244.7M capex.

news.summary: In Los Angeles on May 8, 2025, Gravity, the sole American OEM independent EVSE hardware and software developer, revealed their latest charging technology. The 500kW charging system, almost twice as fast as the closest competitors in LA, stands out for its speed. Their charging sites are uniquely designed with Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) capabilities to enhance resiliency. Gravity also introduced the industry's first human-less charging system tailored for autonomous vehicles.
news.headline: Gravity to introduce 5-minute EV charging at 8 LA sites, 500kW - twice as fast as rivals. Includes V2G technology and autonomous vehicle charging.

news.summary: On May 8, 2025, in St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, Fortis Inc. held its Annual Meeting of Shareholders. The company (TSX/NYSE: FTS) disclosed that shareholders unanimously supported all agenda items. This included the election of nominated directors, the appointment of Deloitte LLP as auditors, and the endorsement of the Corporation's executive compensation approach through a non-binding advisory vote. This decisive outcome underscores strong investor confidence in Fortis' leadership and financial stewardship.
news.headline: Fortis Inc. shareholders approve directors, auditors, and executive compensation in annual meeting.

news.summary: In Scottsdale, Arizona, Nextiva will be celebrating its 17th anniversary on May 15th by marking the inaugural Customer Experience Day. This event aims to highlight the role of AI-driven technology in transforming customer interaction. Nextiva, a prominent player in unified customer experience management, will emphasize the significance of reinventing customer engagement methods. Customer Experience Day will be held annually on May 15th, recognizing individuals shaping the future of customer relations.
news.headline: Nextiva launches Customer Experience Day on May 15, their 17th anniversary, celebrating AI-driven technology enhancing customer engagement globally.

news.summary: Recently, the Mars family of brands introduced a new product for dogs called PEDIGREE® DRIZZLERS™ Sauce in Franklin, Tennessee. This mealtime sauce is designed to bring bold flavors to every bite for our four-legged friends. The launch took place on May 8, 2025.
news.headline: PEDIGREE® launches new DRIZZLERS™ Sauce for dogs, adding bold flavors to meals. Released on May 8, 2025 by Mars.

news.summary: A report by Solidus Labs revealed that 98.7% of tokens on Solana’s Pump.Fun DEX are linked to pump-and-dump schemes or rug pulls, with 93% of liquidity pools on Raydium showing signs of soft rug pulls. These fraudulent activities have brought attention to the risks facing traders and institutions in Solana's meme coin market. Despite Solana's efficiency and popularity for meme coin trading, scammers exploit its features to execute fraudulent schemes rapidly. Pump.Fun, with a daily trading volume over $100 million, deployed over 7 million tokens between January 2024 and March 2025, but only 97,000 tokens have liquidity above $1,000. The report urges enhanced monitoring to safeguard against financial and reputational risks in the Solana ecosystem. Full story on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: 98.7% of Pump.Fun tokens on Solana are frauds, with 93% of Raydium liquidity pools showing soft rug pull characteristics. Trading volume exceeds $100M, but only 97,000 tokens maintain liquidity above $1,000. Urgent need for enhanced monitoring to protect traders from risks.

news.summary: Ocugen (NASDAQ: OCGN) is set to release its quarterly earnings report on May 9, 2025. Analysts predict an earnings per share (EPS) of $-0.06. In the last quarter, Ocugen missed EPS estimates by $0.00, resulting in a 9.96% increase in share price the following day. As of May 7, Ocugen shares were trading at $0.6621, down 56.46% over the last 52 weeks. Analysts recommend a Buy rating for Ocugen based on 4 analyst ratings. Investors are hopeful for positive guidance and exceeding estimates in the upcoming announcement, understanding the impact of future projections on stock prices.
news.headline: Ocugen's upcoming earnings report predicts EPS of $-0.06 on May 9, investors hopeful for exceeding estimates. Stock price past performance influenced by future projections. Shares trading at $0.6621, down 56.46% over last 52-week period. Consensus rating for Ocugen is Buy from 4 analysts.

news.summary: Miami, May 8, 2025 - Nsight Health, a top provider of Remote Patient Monitoring and Chronic Care Management services, has recently formed a partnership with Zoo Health, a leading medical billing company catering to healthcare providers nationwide. This collaboration aims to enhance services offered to healthcare providers, ensuring effective patient care and billing processes. The partnership focuses on leveraging technology and expertise to improve patient outcomes and streamline medical billing practices.
news.headline: Nsight Health partners with Zoo Health to expand Remote Patient Monitoring & Chronic Care Management.

news.summary: Today, in Hong Kong on May 8, 2025, Global IBO Group Ltd. ("GIBO") and Bukit Jalil Global Acquisition 1 Ltd. ("BUJA") (NASDAQ: BUJA) finalized their merger. GIBO functions as an all-encompassing AIGC animation streaming service, while BUJA operates as a publicly-traded special purpose acquisition business. The specifics of the agreement and the financial implications have not been disclosed.
news.headline: Global IBO Group Ltd. merges with Bukit Jalil Global Acquisition 1 Ltd., creating AIGC streaming platform.

news.summary: The GENIUS Act, aimed at regulating stablecoins in the US, failed to pass cloture in the Senate on May 8. Sponsored by Senator Bill Hagerty and co-sponsored by Senators Tim Scott, Kirsten Gillibrand, Cynthia Lummis, and Angela Alsobrooks, it faced resistance from Democrats concerned about President Trump's cryptocurrency involvements. The bill included stricter regulations for stablecoin issuers and provisions for Anti-Money Laundering. Despite being a bipartisan effort, it did not garner enough support. Senate Majority Leader John Thune criticized Democrats for not cooperating. This news represents a setback for cryptocurrency regulation in the US.
news.headline: US Stablecoin bill (GENIUS Act) fails cloture in Senate on May 8. Sponsored by Sen. Hagerty with support from Sens. Scott, Gillibrand, Lummis, and Alsobrooks. Democrats withdraw support citing concerns about Trump's crypto ventures. Bill amended to include stricter requirements for issuers, focusing on AML. Failure criticized by Sen. Thune. Developing story.

news.summary: The meme cryptocurrency Popcat (CRYPTO: POPCAT) has surged by 17% within a day, sparking renewed interest in meme coins among traders who anticipate it to compete with popular meme tokens like Dogecoin (CRYPTO: DOGE) and Shiba Inu (CRYPTO: SHIB). A notable crypto trader, Unipcs, disclosed that he has profited seven figures from his Popcat investment, contrasting with potential earnings of $500,000 if he had sold his holdings two weeks ago as suggested by critics. Despite the recent spike, Popcat remains 77% below its peak value. Unipcs remains bullish on the coin, foreseeing further growth amidst the meme coin frenzy and plans to retain his position in anticipation of more gains. Find the complete article on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Popcat surges 17% in a day, trader predicts move to $1, outpacing Dogecoin, Shiba Inu. Trader up seven figures on position. Popcat still down 77% from all-time high. Anticipates major upside.

news.summary: On May 8, 2025, in Los Angeles, Jamie Court, the president of Consumer Watchdog, submitted a letter urging the University of Southern California to probe into the conduct of Michael Mische, a professor at USC Marshall School of Business. Court raised concerns regarding a potential breach of the university's conflict of interest policies by Mische.
news.headline: Consumer Watchdog calls out USC professor Mische for not disclosing ties to Saudi Arabia in advocating for oil producers.

news.summary: A BCC Research study projects the global tissue diagnostics market will grow from $8.9 billion in 2024 to $12.4 billion by 2029, with a 7.0% compound annual growth rate. The report covers segments like histopathology, molecular pathology, and digital pathology, focusing on instruments and reagents. It analyzes markets in North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific, Latin America, the Middle East, and Africa, excluding liquid biopsy. Precision medicine advancements like digital pathology and AI contribute to diagnostic accuracy, crucial for early cancer detection. Factors driving market growth include rising cancer cases, companion diagnostics, early testing importance, awareness campaigns, and aging populations. This data assists companies in decision-making, and policymakers in resource allocation and product development for cancer management.
news.headline: Tissue Diagnostics Market set to hit $12.4B by 2029, growing at 7.0% CAGR. Covers market dynamics, trends, and regional analysis.

news.summary: Viatris Inc. released data from a Phase 3 study on XULANE LO, a low-dose weekly contraceptive patch. The study showed favorable contraceptive efficacy with a Pearl Index of 4.14 and a 3.7% cumulative pregnancy rate over 13 cycles. The patch demonstrated a favorable safety profile with most adverse events reported as mild to moderate. Patch adhesion was strong, with only 1.3% detaching completely over seven days. The company plans to submit a New Drug Application to the FDA. The article also mentions Viatris' $335 million opioid settlement framework.
news.headline: Viatris releases positive data on contraceptive study: XULANE LO patch showed 4.14 Pearl Index, 3.7% pregnancy rate over 13 cycles, FDA submission planned. Stock rises.

news.summary: Teradyne, Inc. (NASDAQ:TER) based in North Reading, Massachusetts, revealed a quarterly cash dividend of $0.12 per share. Shareholders of record on May 22, 2025, will receive the payment on June 13, 2025. Teradyne specializes in designing, developing, and producing automated test equipment and advanced robotics systems. The company, listed as NASDAQ:TER, focuses on providing test solutions for semiconductors and electronic products to assist customers in maintaining their quality standards.
news.headline: Teradyne (NASDAQ: TER) declared a $0.12 quarterly cash dividend to be paid on June 13, 2025, to shareholders of record by May 22.

news.summary: In Hartford, Connecticut, on May 8, 2025, Shipman & Goodwin LLP successfully obtained an order certifying a class consisting of previous residents of Barbour Gardens, a housing complex in Hartford's North End that received public subsidies. The complex was closed down by regulators due to inadequate conditions.
news.headline: Shipman & Goodwin LLP certifies class of former Barbour Gardens tenants; complex shuttered due to substandard conditions.

news.summary: A seasoned cybersecurity expert has joined Edge Delta to enhance security measures and assist in the company's rapid expansion. Headquartered in Seattle, the leading provider of intelligent telemetry pipelines for security and observability data, appointed Joan Pepin on May 8, 2025. Pepin's role will be crucial in bolstering the company's security defense mechanisms and facilitating its growth in the enterprise sector.
news.headline: Edge Delta names Joan Pepin as new CISO to bolster security amid rapid growth. Joining in 2025.

news.summary: ADAR1 Capital Management, LLC has disclosed their plan to abstain from voting for Directors Dr. Mary Ann Gray and Dr. Alpna Seth at Keros Therapeutics (Nasdaq: KROS). The announcement was made on May 8, 2025 via PRNewswire. ADAR1 is the leading stockholder of Keros, which is listed on Nasdaq.
news.headline: ADAR1 Capital Management intends to withhold votes for Keros Therapeutics directors Dr. Mary Ann Gray and Dr. Alpna Seth.

news.summary: An extensive study by BCC Research delves into the impact of advancements, market forces, regional patterns, and ESG initiatives on the women's health therapeutics sector valued at $81.2 billion. Released in Boston on May 8, 2025, the report highlights the increasing global recognition of gender-specific healthcare, signaling a growing focus on addressing the unique needs and challenges faced by women in medical treatment.
news.headline: $81.2 billion women's health therapeutics market analyzed for growth potential due to innovation, investment, and global impact.

news.summary: In Tucson, Arizona, 20th May 2025, Critical Path Institute® (C-Path) is commemorating a significant achievement as it completes two decades of revolutionizing drug development through partnerships, regulatory expertise, and innovative tactics. This milestone signifies a long journey of shaping the drug development sector, advancing towards better pharmaceutical solutions.
news.headline: Critical Path Institute (C-Path) in Tucson celebrates 20 years of advancing drug development through collaboration and innovation.

news.summary: Mars is demonstrating at the 2025 Sweets & Snacks Expo in Indianapolis on May 8, 2025, how changing consumer behaviors and extended seasons are influencing the snacking industry. The company, known for producing popular snacks and treats, is highlighting this shift in consumption patterns.

news.summary: The highly competitive summer internship program for graduate students in the industry will be placing a record-breaking number of students at top-performing funds. Taking place in New York, as of May 8, 2025, Impact Capital Managers (ICM), the prominent association representing private capital fund managers focused on achieving exceptional returns, is facilitating this opportunity.
news.headline: Impact Capital Managers selects 2025 Mosaic Fellows, placing a record number of students into top funds to shape future impact investors.

news.summary: OrthoLazer, Your Orthopedic Wellness Center in Warsaw, Ind., is promoting the therapeutic benefits of light as a noninvasive remedy for pain and inflammation. As part of Arthritis Awareness Month in May 2025, the company urges individuals suffering from these conditions to consider this natural approach. Exploring the healing potential of light can offer relief for those dealing with discomfort and swelling. With a focus on providing a holistic alternative, OrthoLazer advocates for the use of light therapy as a safe and effective means of managing these issues.
news.headline: OrthoLazer promotes using light therapy for pain relief during Arthritis Awareness Month. Located in Warsaw, Ind.

news.summary: 365 Retail Markets, the leading provider of unattended retail technology, has introduced innovative additions to its AI and Computer Vision Product Line in Troy, Michigan. These new options include temperature control, size customization, and age verification features. These modular smart store solutions enhance the company's existing product line and aim to further revolutionize the retail experience. The announcement was made on May 8, 2025, marking a significant advancement for the company as it continues to lead the industry with cutting-edge technology.
news.headline: 365 Retail Markets introduces new modular smart store options to aid operator growth, with AI & computer vision products.
